channel.title: Home on Reo Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Home on Reo Blog

item.title: Digital Twin Simulation Streaming Relay Server
item.description: Goal Build a reliable digital twin service that controls and visualizes a traffic simulation derived from real-world traffic data. The backend sits between the simulation server and the front-end viewer, providing a stable API contract and twin-ready data. What I built Backend orchestrator layer between front-end and simulation server: Front-end uses a single domain API surface, without directly depending on simulation-specific protocols or schemas. Simulation control APIs (domainized): Create/run/stop simulation jobs, query status/progress, and fetch results.

item.title: GOUTILS: Internal Go Shared Utilities
item.description: Summary A shared Go module that reduces repeated infra code and helps new services adopt consistent patterns quickly.

item.title: ODIN Sentinel Server (Realtime Events, BBox, Streaming)
About
item.description: Strengths Solve bottlenecks/drops/memory pressure with system boundaries (queueing, integrity checks, storage/compression) Improve metric trust by filtering physically impossible noise and validating with sampling Reduce repeated infra code via shared libraries (logging/validation/DB pools/network integrations) How I work Diagnose with metrics, iterate via hypothesis → verification → improvement. Validate data against reality, not just against code-level assumptions. Improve team productivity through standardization and reusable components.

item.title: Headway/GAP KPI Pipeline for Signal Ops
item.description: Goal Build reliable Headway/GAP-based traffic-flow metadata (KPIs) and publish them to a shared database so a traffic-signal vendor could use them as indicators for signal operations improvement. What I built Lane-level event processing: group-by lane, sort by timestamp, generate consecutive vehicle pairs. Phase-aware validation: only accept consecutive pairs that belong to the same signal phase window (prevents cross-phase mixing and improves KPI stability). KPI computation: Headway = Δt (seconds) GAP = Headway - (vehicle_length / speed_mps) (unit conversion included) Quality gates: remove noisy/impossible samples (short/long intervals, physically inconsistent GAP), then aggregate per signal cycle (thresholds not disclosed).
